title: Role-protocol linkage updates (kernel_builder / integration_validator / wp_validator / coder / orchestrator / classic_orchestrator / validator)
goal: Add HBR-citation sections to the seven role protocols that govern WPs touching product code: kernel_builder, integration_validator, wp_validator, coder, orchestrator, classic_orchestrator, validator. Each protocol's WP-claim/closeout/handoff section gains a `## HBR Gate Obligations` block citing CX-131 + spec §5.6 + the relevant pillar applicability per role. NOT activation_manager (governance-only) or memory_manager (operator-tooling-only).
expected_diff_shape: Edit existing role protocol files under .GOV/roles/<role>/*_PROTOCOL.md adding the new HBR section; no role-protocol creation or removal; preserve all existing rule IDs and section anchors.
unblock_conditions: MT-013 ModelManual + IPC bridge land so role protocols can cite the manual entries the HBR gates reference.
implementation_notes:
Each of the seven role protocols gets a new top-level `## HBR Gate Obligations` section (insert after the role's primary scope section, before any closeout sections). The section content is consistent across protocols but the cited rules differ by role applicability. KERNEL_BUILDER: cite all 23 HBR rules with focus on INT pillar (implementation responsibility). INTEGRATION_VALIDATOR: cite all 23 with focus on validator-scan integration (MT-005). WP_VALIDATOR: cite the 23 with focus on per-MT verification path (note: per packet workflow.wp_validator_gate=DISABLED for KERNEL-004; the protocol still carries the obligations for other WPs). CODER: cite the INT + QUIET pillars (code-writing focus). ORCHESTRATOR: cite the SWARM + MAN pillars (handoff + manual currency focus). CLASSIC_ORCHESTRATOR: cite the same as ORCHESTRATOR but with the note that classic_orchestrator is manual-relay only and HBR gates apply equally. VALIDATOR (catch-all role): cite all 23. Each section format: `
## HBR Gate Obligations

This role must honor HANDSHAKE_BUILD_RULES.json v1.2.0+ (see Codex CX-131, spec §5.6, registry at .GOV/roles_shared/records/HANDSHAKE_BUILD_RULES.json).

- At WP claim: read packet.acceptance_matrix.hbr and confirm row applicability.
- At MT execution: produce evidence per evidence_kind for each Applicable rule.
- At role handoff: HandoffGate (MT-004) MUST PASS or the handoff is blocked.
- At closeout: confirm no HBR row is PENDING/STEER/BLOCKED per CX-503B1.

Applicable pillars for this role: [INT / SWARM / VIS / QUIET / MAN — list per role].
`. Operator note inline: this MT does NOT touch activation_manager or memory_manager protocols (the brief explicitly excluded them). Tests: a Node script `.GOV/roles_shared/checks/role-protocol-hbr-linkage.mjs` greps each of the seven protocols for `## HBR Gate Obligations` and `CX-131` mentions; fails if absent. Add the script as a gov-check sub-check.
risk_if_missed: Without role-protocol linkage, roles read protocols that don't mention HBR; sessions skip the gate.
validator_focus: All seven role protocols include the section; activation_manager and memory_manager protocols are NOT touched (verify via git diff); existing rule IDs and section anchors preserved.

minimum_controls: Activation_manager and memory_manager protocols are explicitly NOT touched.
Existing rule IDs and section anchors preserved (no renumbering).
Linkage scanner sub-check added to gov-check umbrella.
